question: Does the response strictly follow the requested output sequence: (1) Overall spatial atmosphere rendering, (2) Wall material close-up, (3) Worktable surface material close-up, and (4) Functional zoning floor plan sketch?
Are the wall material and worktable surface material presented as two separate, distinct close-up images rather than a combined collage or single image?
Is the final image a simplified functional zoning floor plan sketch (top-down view) rather than a photorealistic room perspective or complex architectural drawing?
Does the text explicitly explain the reasoning behind the choice of the specific wall material, citing both aesthetic (wabi-sabi) and practical factors?
Does the text explicitly explain the reasoning behind the choice of the specific worktable surface material, citing its suitability for ceramics work (e.g., durability, ease of cleaning)?
Does the text explain the logic behind the circulation flow design, detailing how the layout supports the movement between functional zones (e.g., clay prep → throwing → drying)?
Does the text explicitly address the specific constraints of an attic space (e.g., sloped ceilings) and explain how the skylight is utilized in the design?
Does the overall spatial atmosphere rendering clearly depict an attic space with a visible skylight and recognizable ceramics studio elements (e.g., pottery wheel, shelving)?
Do the material close-up images clearly show detailed surface textures consistent with the wabi-sabi style (e.g., rough plaster, natural wood, stone) rather than generic flat colors?
Does the floor plan sketch clearly label distinct functional zones appropriate for a ceramics studio (e.g., throwing area, glazing area, storage, sink)?
Does the overall spatial rendering visually reflect the 'minimalist wabi-sabi' style described in the text (e.g., earthy tones, natural materials, simplicity)?
Do the visual details in both the wall and worktable close-up images match the specific material descriptions provided in the adjacent text?
Does the floor plan sketch accurately reflect the functional zones and circulation logic described in the text (i.e., zones mentioned in text appear on plan)?
Does the floor plan sketch provide clear spatial information (e.g., relative proportions, circulation paths) beyond just a list of rooms, aiding in spatial understanding?
Are the materials shown in the close-up images visually consistent with the materials visible in the overall spatial atmosphere rendering?
Are the images placed logically adjacent to their relevant text sections (e.g., close-ups near material descriptions, plan near circulation text)?
Are the proposed materials and spatial layout practical and safe for a real-world ceramics studio (e.g., avoiding water-sensitive materials in wet areas)?
Does the proposal demonstrate successful spatial reasoning by effectively integrating the attic's skylight into the functional layout (e.g., placing detailed work areas near the light)?
